checks.name: PetclinicService REST Client interface
checks.details: Interface uses @RegisterRestClient(baseUri="http://localhost:9966"), name ends with Service, uses Jakarta REST annotations (@GET, @Path, @PathParam), implements 3 GET endpoints: listPets, getPet, listPetTypes

checks.name: PetTools MCP Server class
checks.details: Uses @Tool annotation from io.quarkiverse.mcp.server, @ToolArg for parameters, returns ToolResponse with TextContent, injects PetclinicService via @RestClient, serializes results to JSON via ObjectMapper

checks.name: Model DTOs (Pet, PetType, Visit)
checks.details: All three model classes match the OpenAPI spec schemas with correct field types: Pet has id, name, birthDate, type, ownerId, visits; PetType has id, name; Visit has id, petId, date, description

checks.name: Kubernetes manifest sidecar
checks.details: MCP server sidecar container added with name mcp-server, image petclinic-mcp-bob:1.0.0-SNAPSHOT, containerPort 8888. Original petclinic container preserved. Service and Route unchanged.

checks.name: Only GET operations implemented
checks.details: No POST, PUT, DELETE, or PATCH operations present in PetclinicService or PetTools

checks.name: Legacy source unmodified
checks.details: No modifications to the Spring Petclinic REST source; only the Quarkus sidecar project was modified

checks.name: pom.xml dependencies
checks.details: pom.xml already contains quarkus-mcp-server-http and quarkus-rest-client-jackson dependencies. No changes needed.

warnings: The REST client baseUri is hardcoded to http://localhost:9966. In the Kubernetes sidecar deployment, localhost refers to the same Pod, which is correct since both containers share the same network namespace.
ToolResponse.success() API usage assumes Quarkus MCP Server extension version compatible with Quarkus 3.32.1. Verify the exact API signature if compilation issues arise.
